rationale: Acting on Review 1 leaves the public `antipode_convMul_id` (a thin but readable WithConv wrapper around the existing axiom, used once in `convInv_mul_cancel`) and all other reuse intact; acting on Review 2 forces an inlining or `private` change to it. This is the reviews' only substantive difference and is a minor/stylistic nit that no maintainer would insist on for the reuse rubric (the lemma does not duplicate any public declaration and improves proof ergonomics).
